symptom: a hard painless bump near the knee that has grown for years and now catches on things
hook: a bony spur was jutting off the shaft with its shell flowing straight into the parent bone like a branch
whatYouSee: A bony spur jutting off the shaft of the bone with its own cortex flowing straight into the parent bone like a branch off a tree.
whyItMatters: A cap of cartilage kept quietly adding bone to one spot for years the same way normal growth plates do until it formed its own antler-like spur.
treatment: Leave it alone if it stays quiet and remove it surgically only when it hurts or catches or starts to change.
takeaway: A bone can grow its own second bone right out of its side.

generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in with a hard painless bump near the knee that has grown for years and now catches on things.

Then the X-ray loaded 😭

And a bony spur was jutting off the shaft with its shell flowing straight into the parent bone like a branch.

Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻

What's the most likely diagnosis?

Wild guesses are welcome 👀
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Osteochondroma

👀 What you see:
A bony spur jutting off the shaft of the bone with its own cortex flowing straight into the parent bone like a branch off a tree.

🦴 Why it matters:
A cap of cartilage kept quietly adding bone to one spot for years the same way normal growth plates do until it formed its own antler-like spur.

💊 Treatment:
Leave it alone if it stays quiet and remove it surgically only when it hurts or catches or starts to change.

laypersonQuestion: Would you notice this bump walking around?
seedHint: Look at where the spur meets the main bone and ask yourself if that connection looks planned or accidental.
